item.title: Central African Republic: Elections in one of the world's poorest nations | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Voters in the Central African Republic go to the polls on Sunday in nationwide elections. They'll be casting ballots for president and for lawmakers at all levels. The country is still recovering from civil wars over a decade ago. Today the Central African Republic remains one of the world's poorest nations.
00:00 Central African Republic election: What's at stake? 04:16 Lewis Mudge, Central Africa Director, Human Rights Watch
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy on his way for talks with counterpart Trump in Florida
item.author: euronews
item.content:encoded:
Ukrainian President Zelenskyy on his way for talks with counterpart Trump in Florida
Volodymyr Zelenskyy left Poland early on Saturday to meet for talks with US President Donald Trump on ending the war in Ukraine, after a stopover in Canada.
READ MORE : http://www.euronews.com/2025/12/27/ukrainian-president-zelenskyy-on-his-way-for-talks-with-counterpart-trump-in-florida

item.title: Massive pile up involving more than 50 vehicles in Japan | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
In Japan, a massive crash involving 67 vehicles in snowy weather killed two people and injured 26 others over the weekend, authorities say.
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: One dead and scores of people injured after Russia launches massive attack on Kyiv
item.author: euronews
item.content:encoded:
One dead and scores of people injured after Russia launches massive attack on Kyiv
The latest attack comes as presidents Zelenskyy and Trump prepare to meet in Florida on Sunday amid ongoing efforts to end the war.
READ MORE : http://www.euronews.com/2025/12/27/one-dead-and-scores-of-people-injured-after-russia-launches-massive-attack-on-kyiv

item.title: Russian drone attacks ‘designed to break the will of the people’ – former Ukraine adviser
item.author: Ayshah Tull
item.content:encoded: We spoke to Yuriy Sak – former advisor to the Ukrainian Defence Minister.

item.title: Zelenskyy to propose 60-day ceasefire in Trump meeting
item.author: Harry Fawcett
item.content:encoded: Volodymyr Zelenskyy is already on his way to Canada for talks with western allies ahead of tomorrow’s meeting with Mr Trump.He told reporters Ukraine was “doing everything it can to stop this war”.Not so Russia, which launched an onslaught on Kyiv, targeting the city with missiles and drones through the night and into this morning.Russia’s defence ministry says it’s downed over 100 drones in a Ukrainian counter attack.

item.title: Thailand and Cambodia agree to new ceasefire deal
item.author: Jane Deith
item.content:encoded: Thailand and Cambodia have signed a ceasefire to end weeks of fierce fighting along their border – which forced a million people to flee their homes.The 72 hour truce was agreed by the two countries after an intervention by China.Donald Trump had claimed much of the credit for a previous ceasefire deal which collapsed last month.

item.content:encoded:
One dead and scores of people injured after Russia launches massive attack on Kyiv
The latest attack comes as presidents Zelenskyy and Trump prepare to meet in Florida on Sunday amid ongoing efforts to end the war.

item.title: Russia launches massive missile and drone strikes on Kyiv | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Russia does 'not want to end the war’: Nearly 500 drones and 40 missiles hit Kyiv and other regions overnight targeting energy and civilian infrastructure, Zelenskyy said.

item.title: China sanctions 20 American defence companies and 10 executives over arms sales to Taiwan
item.author: euronews
item.content:encoded:
China sanctions 20 American defence companies and 10 executives over arms sales to Taiwan
According to the Chinese foreign ministry, the sanctions entail freezing the companies’ assets in China and banning individuals and organisations from accessing them.

item.title: Thailand and Cambodia sign new ceasefire agreement to end weeks of deadly border fighting
item.author: euronews
item.content:encoded:
Thailand and Cambodia sign new ceasefire agreement to end weeks of deadly border fighting
The ceasefire, which came into force around noon local time, calls for no further military movements by either side and no violations of either side’s airspace for military purposes.
READ MORE : http://www.euronews.com/2025/12/27/thailand-and-cambodia-sign-new-ceasefire-agreement-to-end-weeks-of-deadly-border-fighting

item.title: Myanmar's junta holds first general election in 5 years: Why are critics questioning its legitimacy?
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
People in Myanmar are preparing to head to the polls this weekend, amid a civil war and one of Asia's worst humanitarian crises. Sunday's poll is the first of three phases of the election, which has been heavily criticized by the United Nations and Southeast Asia's ASEAN bloc. Opponents say the election is an attempt by the ruling military junta to win legitimacy on the global stage. The vote is further complicated by the junta's lack of control over all of Myanmar's territory. It's fighting a civil war that according to UN estimates has killed more than 6,000 civilians and displaced 3.6 million people.
Chapter Breakdown 0:00 Political situation in Myanmar 2:13 Mark Farmaner, Director of Burma Campaign UK, on the junta's goal in holding these elections
#myanmarelection #myanmarjunta #humanitariancrisismyanmar
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

Für Videos in deutscher Sprache besuchen Sie: https://www.youtube.com/dwdeutsch
Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/user/deutschewelleenglish?sub_confirmation=1

item.title: Why does loneliness and isolation particularly affect the younger generations? | DW Reporter
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Young people are more connected than ever – but have never felt so isolated. Why does loneliness particularly affect the generation under 30?
Studies show that almost half of young adults feel lonely. A few years ago, the figures were significantly lower. We spend time with Lalena, a DJ with thousands of social media followers, who parties with the crowds at night and fears the silence of the morning after. We meet Sean, who lost his way in the world of video games before realizing he had to make a fresh start. And we talk to Jana and Noah, looking for support after personal loss. Experts warn that social media can’t create or replace real human interaction. And they encourage young people to recognize that loneliness isn’t a sign of weakness. It is human. What helps? Openness, genuine encounters – and sometimes music or sport.
Chapter 00:00 Why do young people feel lonely? 00:25 Lalena: DJ life between glamor and silence 02:05 Social media & loneliness: the deception 03:35 Creativity as therapy: music to relieve isolation 05:15 From gaming to a new sense of community 07:45 Ask the experts: does media consumption make us lonely? 09:05 Grief and the search for support 10:45 Young Supporters: a group effort 11:35 What really helps: sport, openness, genuine encounters 12:15 Loneliness is human — not a weakness
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: America is 'Hopelessly Divided' Headed Into 2026, Says Frank Luntz
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
Frank Luntz, pollster and CEO of FIL Inc., says America is 'hopelessly divided' by political affiliation as he reflects on 2025 and what he expects from an equally divided Congress of 2026. -------- Watch Bloomberg Radio LIVE on YouTube Weekdays 7am-6pm ET WATCH HERE: http://bit.ly/3vTiACF

item.title: What are the terms of the new Thailand-Cambodia ceasefire agreement? | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Thailand and Cambodia have agreed to a ceasefire to end weeks of deadly border clashes. In a joint statement, both sides have promised to freeze troop movements and allow civilians living in border areas to return home. A long-standing border conflict between the neighboring countries reignited this month. The fighting is believed to have killed more than 100 and displaced hundreds of thousands of people. The dispute stems largely from a century-old map, created when Cambodia was still under French colonial rule.
Chapter Breakdown 0:00 Thailand and Cambodia agree to truce 0:38 Tommy Walker, journalist reporting from Chanthaburi, Thailand, on whether the deal is likely to hold
#thaicambodiaborder #thaicambodiaceasefire #borderdisputethai
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.author: euronews
item.content:encoded:
AFCON 2025 heats up as Morocco, Egypt shine and fan zones come alive
The energy is contagious, and that passion is driving the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations. Groups A and B returned to action yesterday for the first day of the second round with the host nation Morocco also involved.

item.title: China's 'nuclear move' after Trump's "Liberation Day" | Berlin Briefing Podcast
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
2025 has exposed Germany's dependence on China, as a source of rare earths and as a market and production site for its carmakers and other major industries. Berlin says it's committed to "de-risking" the relationship. But is it serious? Or is it in too deep? As Chancellor Friedrich Merz prepares to visit Xi Jinping in early 2026, Richard Walker sat down with experts Noah Barkin and Clifford Coonan to analyze Germany's options.
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Russia bombards Ukraine with missiles and drones ahead of Trump-Zelenskyy meeting Sunday | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
At least 11 people have been injured in the Ukrainian capital Kyiv after Russia launched a major missile and drone attack on the city. An air raid alert was in force in Kyiv overnight, as the mayor warned of explosions and told people to stay in shelters. Ukraine's Air Force confirmed Russian drones were targeting Kyiv along with regions in the northeast and south. The strikes come a day before Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is set to meet with US President Trump in Florida.
DW Special Correspondent Nimisha Jaiswal reports from Kyiv.
#trumpzelenskyy #ukraine #russiandrones
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

item.title: Did 2025 turn out as bad as economists have been forecasting?
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded: On this episode of the Everybody's Business podcast, Felix Salmon (Slate Money), Robert Smith (Business History, Planet Money) and Tracy Alloway (Odd Lots), join @svaneksmith and @mchafkin to discuss the impact of tariffs and AI on the economy and what we should expect in 2026 https://t.co/SkK727QEvR

item.title: Inside a new plan to bring tech talent into the federal government
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded: If you're a high-skilled tech worker, then potentially huge fortunes await you working for a startup or one of the booming AI giants. But the government needs these types of workers too. On this episode of the Odd Lots podcast, Scott Kupor, director of the US Office of Personnel Management, joins Tracy Alloway and Joe Weisenthal to unpack his new plan to attract top talent for a two-year stint of solving problems across the bureaus http://apple.co/4lTgFT6

item.title: Bringing High-Speed Internet to South Africa's Townships
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
Access to fast, affordable internet is still out of reach for millions of South Africans, especially in townships. For nearly a quarter of the country's residents, digital infrastructure has changed little since the end of Apartheid. Startup Fibertime is seeking to bring high-speed connectivity to communities that have been left behind. -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

item.title: Looking at why people become weird after they become billionaires
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded: From Elon Musk to Bryan Johnson to Alex Karp, the current class of billionaires is nothing short of eccentric. Felix Salmon (Slate Money), Robert Smith (Business History, Planet Money) and Tracy Alloway (Odd Lots), join @svaneksmith and @mchafkin on the Everybody's Business podcast to discuss the world's richest people and what their behavior says about our society https://t.co/SkK727QEvR

item.title: Nigeria confirms US help in fight against terrorism | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Nigeria has denied that the US military's Christmas Day airstrikes were targeting a specific religion. The US launched several attacks against Islamic State militants in northwestern Nigeria. President Trump said the strikes aimed to protect Christians who he claimed were being 'slaughtered' by the militants. However, the Nigerian government, which cooperated with the US action, claims that Islamist insurgents target both Christians and Muslims.
Chapters: 0:00 Nigeria denies religion determined US strike targets 2:50 Miriam Adah, Assistant Research Manager Africa at the NGO ACLED
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

Snow Forecast delivers the latest snow conditions and forecasts directly from the slopes of ski resorts across Europe and Central Asia. (Broadcast 2025/12/27)

item.title: Trump orders attacks on ISIS targets in Nigeria #politics #shorts
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded: The US launched a military strike in Nigeria against Islamic State targets in a security and intelligence collaboration with the African nation's government. President Donald Trump said he directed American forces to carry out "a powerful and deadly strike" against ISIS, and Nigeria's foreign affairs ministry confirmed the "precision hits on terrorist targets." Laura Davison reports.

item.title: Baby Bonds vs. Trump Accounts
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
A new savings vehicle, dubbed “Trump accounts,” is designed to help the rising generation of American children build wealth into adulthood. Under the multitrillion-dollar tax and spending bill signed by President Donald Trump in July, the federal government will contribute $1,000 to accounts set up for every American baby born in the next few years. Darrick Hamilton, Henry Cohen Professor of Economics and Urban Policy at the New School, is behind a comparable concept known as "baby bonds," which he sees as a means to granting more social mobility and agency to young people regardless of family income levels. Professor Hamilton compares and contrasts his idea with the Trump administration's mechanism with Alexis Christoforous and Isabelle Lee on Bloomberg Businessweek Daily. -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets
Like this video? Subscribe and turn on notifications so you don't miss any videos from Bloomberg Markets & Finance: https://tinyurl.com/ysu5b8a9 Visit http://www.bloomberg.com for business news & analysis, up-to-the-minute market data, features, profiles and more.

item.title: Ellison, Paramount Put Pressure on WBD for a Deal
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
Warner Bros. Discovery shares slid early Friday in premarket trading after a report from the New York Post said that Paramount Skydance could walk away from its $30-per-share cash bid and instead litigate against the company’s board for how it handled the process. But with Larry Ellison personally backing the Paramount bid, the pressure is still on. Bloomberg's Chris Palmeri reports. -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets
Like this video? Subscribe and turn on notifications so you don't miss any videos from Bloomberg Markets & Finance: https://tinyurl.com/ysu5b8a9 Visit http://www.bloomberg.com for business news & analysis, up-to-the-minute market data, features, profiles and more.

item.title: Zelenskiy Plans Sunday Meeting with Trump on Peace Plan | Balance of Power 12/26/2025
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
"Balance of Power" focuses on the intersection of politics and global business. On today's show, Fmr. US Representative (D-CA) and House Intelligence Committee Fmr. Ranking Member Jane Harman, Macy’s Tony Spring, Stonecourt Capital and Bloomberg Contributor Rick Davis, FTC and Democratic Strategist Doug Farrar, Fmr. US Ambassador to Ukraine and Atlantic Council Eurasia Center SNR. Director John Herbst, Bank of America’s Brian Moynihan, and Kiss Band's Gene Simmons.
Chapters: 00:02:08 - Eric Martin, Bloomberg News 00:06:26 - Fmr. US Representative (D-CA) and House Intelligence Committee Fmr. Ranking Member Jane Harman 00:15:29 - Laura Davison, Bloomberg News 00:17:37 - Macy’s Tony Spring 00:24:59 - Mike Dorning, Bloomberg News 00:30:44 - Josh Saul, Bloomberg News 00:33:00 - Stonecourt Capital and Bloomberg Contributor Rick Davis 00:34:55 - FTC and Democratic Strategist Doug Farrar 00:38:04 - Vonnie Quinn, Bloomberg News 00:52:32 - Fmr. US Ambassador to Ukraine and Atlantic Council’s John Herbst 01:02:40 - Bank of America’s Brian Moynihan 01:14:26 - Erik Larson, Bloomberg News 01:19:18 - Kiss Gene Simmons -------- Watch Bloomberg Radio LIVE on YouTube Weekdays 7am-6pm ET WATCH HERE: http://bit.ly/3vTiACF
Follow us on X: https://twitter.com/BloombergRadio

item.title: Gold, Silver Smash Records as End-Of-Year Rally Continues
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
Gold, silver, and platinum extended a historic end-of-year rally for precious metals, jumping to all-time highs, with spot gold peaking above $4,530 an ounce. Tatiana Darie has more on Bloomberg Television. -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

item.title: Snow Storm Brings Holiday Travel Disruptions to NYC Airports
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
New York’s major airports have seen hundreds of flight cancellations as a significant winter storm descends on the city and neighboring regions. New York City is in line to get 5 to 9 inches (13 to 23 cm) of snow Friday into Saturday, according to the National Weather Service. Bill Wadell of AccuWeather has the details on Bloomberg Television. -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets
Like this video? Subscribe and turn on notifications so you don't miss any videos from Bloomberg Markets & Finance: https://tinyurl.com/ysu5b8a9 Visit http://www.bloomberg.com for business news & analysis, up-to-the-minute market data, features, profiles and more.

item.title: Tesla Faces New Door Handle Probe
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded: Tesla is facing a new US government probe over the door handles of its Model 3 sedans, people familiar with the matter tell Bloomberg. Steve Westly, a former Tesla board member and managing partner of The Westly Group, speaks with Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg Tech."

item.title: US Aggression Near Venezuela is 'Oil Based,' Says Denver Riggleman
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
Former Representative Denver Riggleman said that recent moves by the US military to sure up capabilities around Latin America are driven by oil and not drug trafficking, as the Trump administration ramps up aggression in the region. Riggleman also gives his perspective on the divisions within the Republican party as it contends differing views on the Epstein files and international conflict. -------- Watch Bloomberg Radio LIVE on YouTube Weekdays 7am-6pm ET WATCH HERE: http://bit.ly/3vTiACF
Follow us on X: https://twitter.com/BloombergRadio

item.title: Holiday Spending Was Practical, Predictable Says Julia Wilson
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
Julia Wilson, principal of consumer and retail strategy at KPMG, said that practical items like sweaters and gift cards were at the top of consumers' shopping lists this holiday season. Wilson also said that for tricky post-holiday tasks like returning or exchanging gifts, consumers are turning to chatbots for help. -------- Watch Bloomberg Radio LIVE on YouTube Weekdays 7am-6pm ET WATCH HERE: http://bit.ly/3vTiACF
Follow us on X: https://twitter.com/BloombergRadio

item.title: Reasonable to be Optimistic About Markets in 2026, Says Rick Gardner
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
Rick Gardner, CIO of RGA Investments, breaks down his outlook heading into the new year. Garnder says that its reasonable for investors to be 'optimistic' about markets in 2026, expecting uncertainty around policy to die down. Gardner said that as tech trade narrows, there is opportunity for investors to diversify outside of the sector. -------- Watch Bloomberg Radio LIVE on YouTube Weekdays 7am-6pm ET WATCH HERE: http://bit.ly/3vTiACF
Follow us on X: https://twitter.com/BloombergRadio

item.title: California braces for more extreme weather | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
The US state of California is bracing for even more extreme weather after wind, rain and snow pummeled the state. At least two people were killed in storm-related incidents. Officials are warning the danger is not over with flash flooding and avalanches possible.
00:00 California hit by powerful storm 02:11 Mathew Cappucci, Meteorologist
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

#California #floods

item.title: The Year, According to Tracy Alloway, Felix Salmon, and Robert Smith
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded: Elon Musk has been advocating for people to have more babies. Many countries like Japan and Germany offer financial rewards to encourage childbirths. Should the rest of the world be doing the same? On this episode of the Everybody's Business podcast, Felix Salmon (Slate Money), Robert Smith (Business History, Planet Money) and Tracy Alloway (Odd Lots), join @svaneksmith and @mchafkin to discuss the declining global fertility rate https://t.co/SkK727QEvR

item.title: Fmr. Rep. Harman on Russia and Ukraine, Middle East, Nigeria
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
Jane Harman, Fmr. US Representative (D-CA) and House Intelligence Committee Fmr. Ranking Member, says she is pessimist because I don’t see that Putin will be satisfied with this deal. She speaks with Joe Mathieu on “Balance of Power.” -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

item.title: Zelenskiy to Meet Trump on Sunday in Florida to Discuss Peace Plan
item.author: Bloomberg Television
item.content:encoded:
John Herbst, former U.S. Ambassador to Ukraine and senior director at the Atlantic Council’s Eurasia Center, says negotiations between the U.S. and Ukraine have made progress, but notes that Vladimir Putin has rejected every proposal so far. Speaking with Joe Mathieu on "Balance of Power," Herbst adds that China is closely watching the war and does not want Putin to lose. -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets

item.title: Zelenskyy to travel to Florida for Trump meeting | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says he'll meet with US President Donald Trump in Florida on Sunday, as diplomatic efforts to end the Russia-Ukraine war intensify. The US media outlet Axios reports that Zelensky is ready to hold a referendum on a potential peace plan. That would allow Ukrainains to approve concessions now prohibited by law, like giving up territory.
00:00 Zelenskyy to travel to Florida for Trump meeting 04:07 Anna Marveeva, Russia Institute, King's College London
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

#Russia #Ukraine #Trump

item.title: Taiwan holds drill to boost response after metro stabbing | DW News
item.author: DW News
item.content:encoded:
Authorities in Taiwan aren't taking chances following a deadly stabbing earlier this month. As part of a security drill, police and firefighters tested their level of preparedness during a simulated mass attack inside Taipei's metro system.
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/
